title: Library Management System
description:
Build a console-based Library Management System that supports the following operations:

1. ADD_BOOK <bookId> <title> <author> <copies>
   - Adds a new book to the library with the given number of copies.
   - If the book already exists, add the copies to the existing count.

2. REGISTER_MEMBER <memberId> <name>
   - Registers a new library member.
   - If the member already exists, print: "MEMBER_ALREADY_EXISTS"

3. BORROW <memberId> <bookId>
   - The member borrows one copy of the book.
   - If the member doesn't exist, print: "MEMBER_NOT_FOUND"
   - If the book doesn't exist, print: "BOOK_NOT_FOUND"
   - If no copies are available, print: "NO_COPIES_AVAILABLE"
   - If the member already has this book, print: "ALREADY_BORROWED"
   - On success, print: "BORROWED_SUCCESSFULLY"

4. RETURN <memberId> <bookId>
   - The member returns the book.
   - If the member hasn't borrowed this book, print: "NOT_BORROWED"
   - On success, print: "RETURNED_SUCCESSFULLY"

5. SEARCH <keyword>
   - Searches books by title or author (case-insensitive, partial match).
   - For each match, print: "<bookId> | <title> | <author> | Available: <availableCopies>"
   - If no matches, print: "NO_RESULTS_FOUND"

6. LIST_BORROWED <memberId>
   - Lists all books currently borrowed by the member.
   - For each book, print: "<bookId> | <title>"
   - If none, print: "NO_BOOKS_BORROWED"
   - If the member doesn't exist, print: "MEMBER_NOT_FOUND"

7. BOOK_STATUS <bookId>
   - Prints: "<title> | Total: <totalCopies> | Available: <availableCopies> | Borrowed by: <member1>, <member2>, ..."
   - If no one has borrowed it, the borrowed-by list should say "None"
   - If the book doesn't exist, print: "BOOK_NOT_FOUND"

Input Format:
- First line: N (number of commands)
- Next N lines: one command per line as described above

Output Format:
- Print the output for each command that produces output (BORROW, RETURN, SEARCH, LIST_BORROWED, BOOK_STATUS, and REGISTER_MEMBER when duplicate).
- Commands ADD_BOOK and successful REGISTER_MEMBER produce no output.

Design Requirements:
- Use proper OOP: create classes for Book, Member, and Library.
- Use appropriate data structures (HashMap, ArrayList, etc.).
- Handle edge cases gracefully.
- Write clean, readable code.
sampleInput:
10
ADD_BOOK B001 TheAlchemist PauloCoelho 3
ADD_BOOK B002 CleanCode RobertMartin 2
REGISTER_MEMBER M001 Alice
REGISTER_MEMBER M002 Bob
BORROW M001 B001
BORROW M002 B001
SEARCH code
LIST_BORROWED M001
BOOK_STATUS B001
RETURN M001 B001
sampleOutput:
BORROWED_SUCCESSFULLY
BORROWED_SUCCESSFULLY
B002 | CleanCode | RobertMartin | Available: 2
B001 | TheAlchemist
TheAlchemist | Total: 3 | Available: 1 | Borrowed by: Alice, Bob
RETURNED_SUCCESSFULLY
constraints:
1 <= N <= 1000
Book IDs and Member IDs are unique alphanumeric strings.
Titles and authors are single words (no spaces).
1 <= copies <= 100
